pages.title: Logistics Brokerage for FMCG — Warehouse, Fulfillment, Distribution | Vologis
pages.description: Vologis selects and vets warehouse operators and logistics partners for FMCG companies — high-turnover warehouses, fulfillment and retail distribution, 100% independent.
pages.intro: Fast-moving consumer goods demand high-turnover logistics, short lead times and resilience to seasonal peaks. Vologis helps FMCG companies find and vet warehouse operators, fulfillment partners and carriers ready for large volumes and fast goods flow. We work 100% independently — no supplier commissions, exclusively in the client's interest.
pages.problem_heading: FMCG logistics doesn't forgive slow turnover
pages.problem_text: In FMCG, margins are thin and volumes are enormous — every picking delay, every forecasting error and every suboptimal warehouse choice shows up in costs across thousands of pallets per month. Finding an operator who can handle high turnover, seasonality and retail chain requirements demands market knowledge and tough negotiations.

pages.summary: Logistics brokerage for FMCG is a service in which Vologis selects and vets warehouse operators, fulfillment partners and carriers ready for the high turnover and large volumes typical of fast-moving goods. It covers high-turnover warehouses, seasonal peak handling and distribution compliant with retail chain requirements — 100% independent of suppliers.

faq.q: Can you find a warehouse that handles FMCG seasonal peaks?
faq.a: Yes. We select operators with scalable throughput capacity who can handle periods of high demand — from Christmas peaks to retail promotional events. We verify real operator capability, not just claims.

faq.q: Will the operator meet retail chain requirements?
faq.a: We verify whether the operator meets the requirements of specific retail chains: delivery windows, EDI integrations, palletisation, labelling and SLA levels. This is essential to avoid penalties and rejected deliveries.

faq.q: How quickly can you find a warehouse for FMCG?
faq.a: We typically present initial proposals within 1–2 weeks. Timing depends on location, required volume and product specifics. In urgent situations we move faster.

faq.q: How will an audit help a high-volume FMCG warehouse?
faq.a: In FMCG, even minor picking path optimisation multiplies across thousands of operations. An audit identifies where you're losing time and money — in slotting, layout, picking method — and shows concrete, quantifiable improvements.

faq.q: Will you account for seasonality and peaks?
faq.a: Yes. We design processes that flex for seasonal peaks and promotions, which in FMCG can multiply volume many times. Optimisation must work both in standard periods and at peak.

faq.q: Do you implement recommendations or just audit?
faq.a: We implement. An audit is the starting point — we finish when changes work and the KPI impact is visible. We can implement recommendations with your team or take over implementation coordination.

faq.q: What is a production logistics audit?
faq.a: It's an analysis of material flows inside the plant: line supply with raw materials, component movement, assembly picking and batch tracking. It shows where internal logistics slows production and how to fix it.

faq.q: Will an audit help before investing in automation?
faq.a: Definitely. Automating chaotic processes only entrenches the chaos. An audit before automation orders the flows and shows what's worth automating and what to simplify first — protecting against a costly, poorly matched investment.

faq.q: How will the audit affect production output?
faq.a: Eliminating line supply delays, shortening movements and efficient batch tracking directly translate into output. Minor delays that scatter today add up to real efficiency gains after optimisation.

pages.h1: Operational Consulting & Audit for the Pharmaceutical Industry
pages.intro: Pharmaceutical logistics combines GDP regulatory rigour with operational efficiency pressure. Vologis audits pharmaceutical processes for compliance, cold chain and traceability, while simultaneously identifying inefficiencies and bottlenecks — because compliance and efficiency don't have to be mutually exclusive.
pages.problem_heading: GDP compliance doesn't have to mean inefficiency
pages.problem_text: Many pharma companies treat processes as a compromise: either GDP compliance or efficiency. In reality, poorly designed processes are simultaneously non-compliant and inefficient. An audit shows how to meet regulatory requirements while optimising costs and flows.

scope.text: Verification of processes against Good Distribution Practice and regulatory requirements.

scope.title: Cold chain
scope.text: Analysis of temperature processes and monitoring in storage and distribution.

scope.title: Traceability and serialisation
scope.text: Verification of batch tracking and serialisation handling systems.

scope.title: Optimisation within compliance
scope.text: Identifying inefficiencies that can be removed without breaching GDP requirements.

pages.summary: Operational consulting for pharma is a service in which Vologis audits pharmaceutical processes for GDP compliance, cold chain and traceability, while simultaneously identifying inefficiencies and optimisation opportunities. We show how to meet regulatory requirements and reduce costs — implementing changes until KPIs are achieved.

faq.q: Will the audit verify GDP compliance?
faq.a: Yes. We verify processes against Good Distribution Practice — storage, cold chain, traceability, documentation. We identify compliance gaps before they become a problem during an inspection.

faq.q: Can costs be optimised without breaching GDP?
faq.a: Yes — and this is a frequent scenario. Poorly designed processes can be simultaneously inefficient and regulatory risks. We show where flows can be improved and costs reduced while remaining fully compliant.

faq.q: Do you cover cold chain auditing?
faq.a: Yes. We analyse temperature processes, monitoring and documentation in storage and distribution of temperature-sensitive products, identifying both compliance risks and inefficiencies.

pages.title: Operational Consulting & Audit for Distribution | Vologis
pages.description: Vologis audits and optimises distribution networks — analyses flows, warehouses and transport for distribution companies, reducing costs and improving service levels.
pages.h1: Operational Consulting & Audit for Distribution Companies
pages.intro: Distribution companies live by the efficiency of goods flow — between suppliers, the warehouse and multiple customers. Vologis audits distribution networks, analyses warehouse structure, flows and transport, and delivers recommendations that reduce costs and improve service levels while maintaining inventory control.
pages.problem_heading: In distribution, service cost and stock levels pull in opposite directions
pages.problem_text: Distribution companies balance product availability against the cost of holding it. Too little stock means lost sales; too much means frozen capital. Add the cost of serving multiple customers and the complexity of a distribution network. Without objective analysis, it's easy to optimise one parameter at the cost of another.

scope.title: Distribution network audit
scope.text: Analysis of warehouse structure, flows and distribution points for cost and service level.

scope.title: Inventory optimisation
scope.text: Analysis of stock levels and turnover, ABC/XYZ structure and replenishment policy.

scope.title: Customer service cost
scope.text: Optimising the cost of serving multiple customers and distribution channels.

scope.title: Transport and routing
scope.text: Optimising the transport model and distribution routes.

for_who.title: Distribution companies
for_who.text: When costs are growing faster than revenues and inventory is getting out of control.

for_who.title: Wholesalers
for_who.text: When you serve multiple customers and need optimisation.

for_who.title: Multi-channel distributors
for_who.text: When you combine different sales and distribution channels.

pages.summary: Operational consulting for distribution is a service in which Vologis audits distribution networks — warehouse structure, flows, inventory and transport. We optimise customer service cost and inventory levels simultaneously, improving service levels without freezing capital. We implement recommendations until measurable KPIs are achieved.

faq.q: How do you optimise inventory without losing availability?
faq.a: We analyse turnover, ABC/XYZ structure and replenishment policy to reduce inventory where safe and increase it where needed. The goal is higher availability at lower frozen capital — not a compromise between them.

faq.q: Will the audit cover the cost of serving multiple customers?
faq.a: Yes. We analyse service cost broken down by customer and channel, identifying unprofitable paths and consolidation opportunities. Often it turns out that part of the service generates costs disproportionate to its value.

faq.q: Do you optimise routes and transport?
faq.a: Yes. Optimising the transport model and distribution routes is a frequent area of real savings for distribution companies — we analyse it as part of a broader distribution network audit.

pages.intro: Building a new logistics facility is a decade-long investment — decisions made at the planning stage weigh on operational costs for the entire asset life. Vologis manages logistics facility Greenfield projects end-to-end: from pre-investment analysis and site selection, through masterplan and technology selection, to operations launch and KPI achievement.
pages.problem_heading: A logistics facility is designed once — for over a decade
pages.problem_text: A wrong location, suboptimal layout or poorly chosen technology in a logistics facility is a cost that stays in operations for the full asset life. And coordinating dozens of suppliers without a single point of accountability leads to delays and compromises that reduce the investment's value.

faq.q: What does a logistics facility Greenfield project cover?
faq.a: The full cycle: pre-investment analysis, site selection, masterplan with layout and ROI calculation, supplier and technology selection, realisation oversight and operations launch until KPIs are achieved. One partner, one accountability.

faq.q: When should I bring Vologis into a project?
faq.a: As early as possible — ideally before selecting the site. Location choice is one of the most important stages, and an error at this level costs money for the entire asset life.

faq.q: Do you use drones in the project?
faq.a: Yes. At the site selection stage we provide geodetic surveys and orthophotomaps, and during construction we conduct aerial construction progress monitoring — this is our technological advantage.

pages.problem_text: A warehouse designed without considering the rotation profile, picking paths and growth plans generates unnecessary movements and costs with every operation — for the entire asset life. Oversized automation is dead capital; undersized automation is a bottleneck from day one.

faq.q: How do you design a warehouse layout?
faq.a: We design the layout for the specific rotation profile (ABC/XYZ), volume and growth plans — minimising movements and picking cost. We account for both current needs and forecast growth, so the warehouse doesn't need redesigning in a few years.

faq.q: How much automation does my warehouse need?
faq.a: As much as volume and ROI justify — no more, no less. Oversized automation is dead capital; undersized automation is a bottleneck. We select the automation level objectively, with no supplier ties.

faq.q: Do you select racking and automation independently?
faq.a: Yes, 100% independently. We have no supplier commissions or partnership agreements. We run tenders and compare offers solely on the basis of fit to your needs and genuine return on investment.

pages.problem_text: A distribution centre simultaneously handles multiple directions, channels and order types — from retail chain pallets to individual e-commerce parcels. A design that doesn't account for this complexity and a location optimal for the entire customer network generates transport and service costs for the full asset life.

faq.q: What's the difference between a distribution centre and a warehouse?
faq.a: A distribution centre handles complex, multi-channel flows to multiple customers simultaneously — from retail chain pallets to e-commerce parcels. It requires a design accounting for this complexity and a location optimal for the full network, not just a single point.

faq.q: How do you choose the location for a distribution centre?
faq.a: We analyse the location against the full customer network and distribution costs — the optimal point for one customer is rarely optimal for the whole network. We use data analysis and our own drone survey services to assess sites.

faq.q: Will the centre be ready for growth?
faq.a: We design the centre with scaling and peak handling in mind. We account for forecast volume and customer number growth, so the centre supports development rather than limiting it after a few years.

pages.title: Interim Management in Logistics Restructuring | Vologis
pages.description: Vologis provides interim management for logistics restructuring — enters company structures, reorganises processes and reduces costs in restructuring projects.
pages.intro: Logistics restructuring requires experienced management, the ability to make difficult decisions and an independent perspective that's hard to find inside an organisation in crisis. Vologis provides an interim manager who enters the company's structure, reorganises processes, reduces costs and leads the team through change — with a focus on a concrete result, not building dependency.
pages.problem_heading: Restructuring requires an outsider who can make the tough calls
pages.problem_text: In restructuring, internal managers are often entangled in relationships, politics and the history of decisions — which makes difficult but necessary changes harder. There's also no time to recruit, and sometimes specific restructuring competencies are lacking. An interim manager brings an objective perspective, experience and the ability to act without that baggage.

faq.q: Why an interim manager rather than an internal team?
faq.a: In restructuring, internal managers can be entangled in relationships, politics and decision history, which makes difficult changes harder. An interim manager brings an objective perspective, experience from multiple restructurings and the ability to act without that baggage.

faq.q: How quickly can an interim manager start a restructuring?
faq.a: In situations requiring urgent intervention we can start within days. After an initial diagnosis we agree scope and plan, and enter the company as quickly as possible.

faq.q: What remains after the restructuring is complete?
faq.a: Ordered processes, reduced costs, a new structure and a trained team ready to take the reins. We prepare the company for independent operation — we don't build dependency on our presence.

faq.q: How accurate are drone measurements?
faq.a: With appropriate procedures and reference points we achieve accuracy to a few centimetres. The specific accuracy level is agreed depending on the measurement purpose and project requirements.

faq.q: Is a drone survey cheaper than a traditional one?

faq.q: What format do you deliver the data in?

pages.problem_text: Standard maps and plans don't show the current, real state of a site — its topography, development, surroundings and changes. An orthophotomap fills this gap, delivering a precise, current picture of the terrain, essential for accurate investment and design assessment.

Orthophotomaps are essential for investment assessment and design — delivered in formats ready for use in design tools, for logistics and investment projects.

faq.q: What is an orthophotomap?
faq.a: It's a calibrated photographic map of terrain — combining the readability of an aerial photograph with the accuracy of a geodetic measurement. Unlike a regular photo, an orthophotomap has a uniform scale and precise measurements can be taken from it.

faq.q: What can you use a site orthophotomap for?
faq.a: To assess the real state of a site before investment, as a precise design base, to analyse development and surroundings, and to document terrain condition. It's essential material at the investment decision stage.

faq.q: How current is the orthophotomap?
faq.a: An orthophotomap shows the terrain state on the day of the flight — a real, current picture, unlike archival maps. We can also produce orthophotomaps cyclically to document changes over time.

faq.q: Why a drone inspection rather than traditional?
faq.a: A drone inspects the roof or facade of a large facility in hours, without platforms, scaffolding or exposing people to working at height. It's faster, cheaper and safer, and the photographic documentation is high resolution.

faq.q: What can be inspected by drone on a warehouse?
faq.a: Roof condition, roofing, skylights, facade, structural elements and other hard-to-access facility parts. A drone reaches locations dangerous or inaccessible to traditional inspection methods.

faq.q: Will I get documentation from the inspection?

pages.problem_heading: A contractor's report isn't the same as an objective picture of progress
pages.problem_text: An investor relying solely on contractor reports has no independent confirmation of real work progress. pages.summary: Drone construction monitoring is a service in which Vologis conducts regular, dated aerial documentation of construction work progress — independent of contractor reports. Monitoring gives investors and developers a tool for schedule control, documentation and settlement, with an objective, undeniable picture of progress at every stage of a logistics investment.

faq.q: How often do you conduct construction monitoring?
faq.a: Most often cyclically — regular flights at set intervals (e.g. weekly or monthly) give an objective picture of progress over time. We match the frequency to the pace of works and investor reporting needs.

faq.q: How does drone monitoring surpass a contractor's report?
faq.a: It gives an independent, dated and undeniable picture of real progress — without relying solely on contractor declarations. Discrepancies between schedule and reality are immediately visible, allowing a timely response.

faq.q: Will the documentation help with settlements?
faq.a: Yes. Dated progress documentation is objective material supporting contractor settlements, investor and financier reporting and resolving any disputes about the level of work completion.

pages.industry: market entry / wejście na rynek PL
pages.title: Market Entry Poland — Logistics & Warehouse Advisory | Vologis
pages.description: Vologis supports foreign companies entering the Polish logistics market — location advisory, warehouse selection and logistics process setup.
pages.intro: Poland is the largest logistics market in Central and Eastern Europe — with over 30 million m² of modern warehouse space, a central EU location and a rapidly growing e-commerce sector. Vologis supports foreign companies entering the Polish market: from logistics requirements analysis and location advisory, through warehouse and operator selection, to supporting the full logistics process setup.
pages.problem_heading: Entering a new market without local knowledge is expensive
pages.problem_text: Foreign companies entering Poland often operate on offer-rate assumptions, unfamiliar local zones and an incomplete picture of available operators. Without a local advisor, they overpay, choose suboptimal locations or partner with operators who look good on paper but don't meet operational requirements.

scope.title: Logistics requirements analysis
scope.text: Defining warehouse, transport and service level requirements for the Polish market.

scope.title: Location advisory
scope.text: Identifying optimal warehouse locations based on customer distribution, labour markets and infrastructure.

scope.title: Warehouse and operator selection
scope.text: Independent selection and vetting of warehouse space and logistics operators — no commission from suppliers.

scope.title: Logistics process setup support
scope.text: Supporting the setup of logistics operations in Poland: process design, system integrations and operational launch.

for_who.title: Foreign companies entering Poland
for_who.text: When you're expanding to Poland and need a local logistics advisor.

for_who.title: Companies relocating logistics to Poland
for_who.text: When you're moving warehousing or distribution to Poland (nearshoring).

for_who.title: E-commerce companies serving the Polish market
for_who.text: When you need a fulfillment partner or warehouse for Polish operations.

pages.summary: Market entry Poland is a service in which Vologis supports foreign companies entering the Polish logistics market — from logistics requirements analysis and location advisory through independent warehouse and operator selection to logistics process setup support. We act exclusively in the client's interest, with no commission from operators or landlords.

faq.q: What does market entry support in Poland include?
faq.a: We support the full process: logistics requirements analysis, location advisory, independent warehouse and operator selection, lease negotiation and logistics process setup support. We act as your local logistics partner throughout the entire entry process.

faq.q: Why use a local advisor for entering Poland?
faq.a: Local knowledge of market zones, operators, real benchmark rates and negotiating standards is worth months of research. We condense this into a structured process and ensure you enter negotiations from a position of knowledge, not guesswork.

faq.q: Do you work with companies from specific sectors?
faq.a: Yes. We support companies from manufacturing, trading, e-commerce and distribution — tailoring the scope to the specific logistics requirements of each sector and market entry model.

pages.title: Market Entry Poland for Foreign Manufacturers | Vologis
pages.description: Vologis supports foreign manufacturing companies opening a warehouse or logistics base in Poland — site selection, operator selection and market entry support.
pages.intro: Foreign manufacturers expanding their supply chain to Poland — setting up a production plant, assembly line or logistics base — need a local partner who understands both the logistics market and the specifics of manufacturing operations. Vologis provides end-to-end support: from site selection and warehouse advisory through independent operator selection to logistics process setup.
pages.problem_heading: Setting up a logistics base in a new country requires local knowledge and objectivity
pages.problem_text: Foreign manufacturers often face a choice between large logistics operators offering standardised services at premium prices, and a local market they don't know well enough to navigate independently. Without a local advisor, they overpay, choose suboptimal locations or miss operators who could better serve their specific manufacturing logistics requirements.

scope.title: Site and location analysis
scope.text: Identifying optimal locations for a production plant, assembly or logistics base — considering labour markets, transport access and supply chain economics.

scope.title: Warehouse and logistics operator selection
scope.text: Independent selection of warehouse space and 3PL operators matched to manufacturing logistics requirements — no commission from suppliers.

scope.title: Supply chain setup support
scope.text: Supporting the design and setup of the Polish supply chain: inbound, production supply and outbound distribution.

scope.title: Local representation
scope.text: Acting as a local logistics advisor throughout the market entry process — from first site visit to operations launch.

for_who.title: Foreign manufacturers setting up in Poland
for_who.text: When you're opening a production plant, assembly or logistics base in Poland.

for_who.title: Companies nearshoring to Poland
for_who.text: When you're relocating manufacturing or supply chain operations from Western Europe to Poland.

for_who.title: Manufacturers expanding their EU distribution
for_who.text: When Poland is a strategic logistics hub for serving Central and Eastern European markets.

pages.summary: Market entry Poland for foreign manufacturers is a service in which Vologis supports foreign manufacturing companies setting up a logistics base in Poland — from site and location analysis through independent warehouse and operator selection to supply chain setup support and local representation. We act exclusively in the client's interest, with no commission from operators or landlords.

faq.q: Why Poland as a manufacturing or logistics location?
faq.a: Poland offers competitive labour costs, a central EU location, well-developed logistics infrastructure, access to a large domestic market and proximity to major European markets. For manufacturers nearshoring from Western Europe, Poland is one of the most attractive destinations in the EU.

faq.q: What makes manufacturing logistics different from standard warehousing?
faq.a: Manufacturing logistics requires synchronisation with production schedules, just-in-time supply, component traceability and often specific certifications (GDP, ADR, automotive standards). We select operators and locations that meet these specific requirements, not just standard storage.

faq.q: Do you support the full market entry process?
faq.a: Yes. We support the full process from initial site analysis through warehouse and operator selection, lease negotiation and logistics process setup. We act as your local logistics partner throughout the entire market entry, providing continuity and local knowledge at every stage.

pages.title: Space Commercialisation for 3PL/4PL Operators | Vologis
pages.description: Vologis sources contracts and tenants for 3PL/4PL logistics operators — maximising network occupancy on a success fee model, without a permanent sales team.
pages.intro: Contract logistics operators live from occupancy — every unutilised metre and every vacant capacity unit erodes margin. Vologis sources contracts and tenants for your vacant capacity across your facility network, acting as an outsourced sales team. We operate on a success fee model, fully independently and without commission from other operators.
pages.problem_heading: In contract logistics, occupancy determines margin
pages.problem_text: A 3PL/4PL operator bears the costs of maintaining facilities and teams regardless of whether capacity is fully utilised. Vacant capacity in one network facility, seasonal troughs, a contract that has ended — every such gap reduces profitability. But selling contract services requires time, competencies and a contact network that operational teams typically don't have.

scope.title: 3PL contract sourcing
scope.text: We reach companies seeking logistics services and contract them onto your network's vacant capacity.

scope.title: Outsourced sales team
scope.text: We act as your sales department for contract services — without the cost of building and maintaining your own B2B sales team.

scope.title: Success fee model
scope.text: Our fee is charged only for contracted volumes and signed agreements. You pay for genuinely acquired business.

scope.title: Contract-to-network matching
scope.text: We match clients to your facilities' profiles and locations — sector, storage type, operational requirements — so the contract is profitable, not just filling metres.

for_who.title: 3PL/4PL operators
for_who.text: When you have vacant capacity in your facility network and want to fill it with profitable contracts.

for_who.title: Contract logistics companies
for_who.text: When you need a steady flow of new contracts without building a sales department.

for_who.title: Warehouse networks
for_who.text: When you manage multiple facilities and want to optimise occupancy across the whole network.

pages.summary: Space commercialisation for 3PL/4PL operators is a service in which Vologis sources contracts and tenants for vacant capacity in a contract logistics operator's facility network. We act as an outsourced sales team on a success fee model — the operator pays only for genuinely contracted business, and we match clients to the profiles and locations of their facilities so contracts are profitable.

faq.q: How does this differ from warehouse space commercialisation?
faq.a: Warehouse space commercialisation covers filling vacant pallet volumes in a single facility. The 3PL/4PL version is broader — we source complete contract logistics contracts for an operator with a network of facilities and throughput capacity, not just warehouse space.

faq.q: How is the service billed?
faq.a: On a success fee model — our fee is charged only for contracted volumes and signed agreements. You pay for genuinely acquired business, without the fixed cost of a sales team.

faq.q: Do you source contracts for specific facilities in the network?
faq.a: Yes. We match clients to the profiles and locations of your facilities — sector, storage type, operational requirements — so the contract is profitable and operationally feasible, not just filling vacant metres.

faq.q: Do you act independently?
faq.a: Yes, 100% independently and without commission from other operators. We represent exclusively the interests of the operator for whom we're sourcing contracts.

pages.h1: Strategic Logistics Partner Selection for Manufacturing Companies
pages.intro: Looking for a comprehensive logistics service and distribution solution for your manufacturing operations, but don't want to overpay at corporate giants or run an exhausting tender? We map your processes and connect you with carefully selected mid-size operators — offering more flexibility and often higher service quality. For manufacturing companies, operator selection is free of charge.
pages.problem_heading: The classic logistics tender is often costly and inefficient
pages.problem_text: Manufacturing companies seeking logistics services typically approach the largest market players — which ends in rigid terms, corporate pricing and service far from bespoke. Running a tender in-house consumes time and resources, and without market knowledge it's hard to reach flexible mid-size operators who often offer better quality at lower cost.

faq.a: Logistics brokerage is independent operator or warehouse selection where the client commissions and pays for the service. Partner selection for manufacturing is a free model for manufacturing companies, based on a network of vetted mid-size operators and as an alternative to a classic tender.

faq.q: Why mid-size operators rather than the corporate giants?
faq.a: Mid-size operators offer more flexibility, more committed service and often higher quality at lower cost than corporate giants operating on rigid rate cards and standards.

faq.q: What does the selection process look like?
faq.a: We first map your logistics and distribution processes to define the requirements. Then we select operators matched to those needs and connect you — rather than sending requests blindly to the largest players.

pages.title: Tender Optimisation in Logistics — Alternative to a Classic Tender | Vologis
pages.description: Vologis offers an alternative to the classic logistics operator tender — process mapping, mid-size operator matching, lower costs and less time than a traditional RFP.
pages.intro: The classic logistics operator tender consumes time, ties up teams and often ends with choosing the largest player with the most rigid terms. We offer an alternative: we map your processes and connect you with carefully selected mid-size operators — more flexible, offering higher quality and lower costs than corporate giants.
pages.problem_heading: Tenders favour the largest, not the best
pages.problem_text: Traditional tender procedures favour large operators who can handle the formalities, not those who offer the best fit and quality. The process is time-consuming, formalised and often leads to rigid contracts with corporate pricing. Flexible mid-size operators who could serve you better and cheaper often don't make it into a tender at all.

scope.title: Requirements mapping
scope.text: We analyse processes and real needs to precisely define operator selection criteria — rather than a generic RFQ.

scope.title: Reaching the right operators
scope.text: We target vetted mid-size operators who typically don't appear in classic tenders.

scope.title: Comparison and selection
scope.text: We compare offers on real fit, quality and cost — not just ability to handle tender formalities.

scope.title: Cost and time reduction
scope.text: We shorten the selection process and reach conditions better than corporate rate card pricing.

for_who.title: Companies planning a logistics tender
for_who.text: When you want to select an operator but a classic tender seems time-consuming and suboptimal.

for_who.title: Manufacturing and distribution companies
for_who.text: When you're looking for better fit and cost than corporate giants' offers.

for_who.title: Procurement and logistics teams
for_who.text: When you want to shorten and streamline the logistics partner selection process.

pages.summary: Tender optimisation in logistics is an alternative to the classic operator tender. Instead of a time-consuming procedure favouring the largest players, Vologis maps the client's processes, reaches vetted mid-size operators and compares them on real fit, quality and cost — shortening the process and achieving conditions better than corporate pricing.

faq.q: How does tender optimisation differ from a classic tender?
faq.a: A classic tender is a formalised procedure that favours large operators who can handle the formalities. Tender optimisation is about reaching vetted mid-size operators and comparing them on real fit, quality and cost — faster and with better results.

faq.q: Is this the same as logistics partner selection for manufacturing?
faq.a: They're related. Partner selection for manufacturing companies is specifically for manufacturing firms and their supply chains. Tender optimisation is a broader take on the operator selection process as an alternative to classic tendering — for manufacturing and distribution companies.

faq.q: Why are mid-size operators sometimes better?
faq.a: They offer more flexibility, more committed service and often higher quality at lower cost than corporate giants operating on rigid standards and rate cards.

faq.q: Will I save time compared to running my own tender?
faq.a: Yes. We take over requirements mapping, reaching out to operators and comparing offers, shortening a process that, run independently, consumes team time and often misses the best operators.
